title: Fritter Fun on December 2!
story: Every December 2, people across America celebrate National Fritters Day by making and eating these crispy, golden treats! Fritters are small pieces of dough that get deep-fried until they're perfectly crunchy on the outside and soft inside. Bakeries sell apple fritters, corn fritters, and banana fritters, while home cooks experiment with everything from zucchini to pineapple. Social media fills up with photos of homemade creations and favorite recipes passed down through families.
activity_question: What happens to fritters when they're cooked?
activity_choices: They get deep-fried until crispy and golden
They're baked in the oven like cookies
They turn purple and start dancing
activity_answer: They get deep-fried until crispy and golden
follow_up_question: What unusual ingredient would you put in a fritter?

title: Clean Air, Clean Future Day
story: December 2 is National Pollution Control Day, when people across the country focus on protecting our planet from harmful pollution. Schools organize clean-up drives where kids pick up trash in parks and neighborhoods. Families plant trees, ride bikes instead of driving, and learn about recycling. Many people share tips on social media about reducing waste and saving energy. It's a day to remember that small actions can make a big difference for cleaner air and water!
activity_question: What do schools often organize on National Pollution Control Day?
activity_choices: Clean-up drives in parks and neighborhoods
activity_answer: Clean-up drives in parks and neighborhoods
follow_up_question: What's one thing your family could do to help reduce pollution?
